grammars.name: Alan Leo's Astrology
grammars.description: Classical astrological interpretations from Alan Leo (1860-1917), the father of modern astrology

grammars.name: Archetypal Astrology — planetary pairs, after Tarnas
grammars.description: Archetypal Astrology — the planets in relationship, not sign by sign but pair by pair

grammars.name: The Aspects, Commented — Ptolemy · Lilly · Canonical
grammars.description: The five classical (Ptolemaic) aspects — conjunction, sextile, square, trine, opposition — each as an item carrying tradition-sourced commentary from two public-domain voices plus a contemporary synthesis

grammars.name: Astro of All Astros — every voice, one placement
grammars.description: A generated meta-voice grammar aggregating every interpretation grammar in this repo into one navigable set: one item per planet, sign, and house, each carrying every voice's own reading of that placement side by side —

grammars.name: The Big Three — a reflection casting
grammars.description: A three-position casting that mirrors the tarot three-card spread so it works in the oracle from day one — but nothing here is drawn at random: every position resolves to an aspect of YOUR OWN chart (Sun, Moon, Ascendant

grammars.name: One Aspect — the smallest casting
grammars.description: The minimal casting, and the purest statement of this channel's rule: one position, filled by ANY single aspect of your own chart — a planet in its sign, a house and what stands in it, or an aspect between two planets

grammars.name: The Twelve Houses — a chart-wheel casting
grammars.description: The chart-shaped casting: the twelve houses as twelve fixed positions on the wheel — the richer target the three-position casting graduates into

grammars.name: Essential Dignities & Rulerships — the traditional scheme, planet by planet and sign by sign

grammars.name: Historiographies of Astrology
grammars.description: A library of the major historiographical VIEWS of astrology — the eras and stances through which humans have read meaning into the sky, each presented faithfully in its own terms and held honestly to the record
